title: India History and Freedom Movement
description: A topic pack covering ancient India, the freedom struggle, and key historical moments.

questions.prompt: Who is known as the Father of the Nation in India?
questions.hint: He led the non-violent freedom movement.

questions.prompt: In which year did India gain independence?
questions.hint: It was two years after the end of World War II.

questions.prompt: Which movement did Mahatma Gandhi launch in 1942?
questions.hint: It urged the British to leave India.

questions.prompt: Who gave the slogan 'Give me blood, and I will give you freedom'?
questions.hint: He also led the Indian National Army.

questions.prompt: Which massacre took place in Amritsar in 1919?
questions.hint: It happened at a public garden.

questions.prompt: Who was the first Prime Minister of independent India?
questions.hint: He was also called Panditji.

questions.prompt: Which Mughal emperor built the Taj Mahal?
questions.hint: He built it in memory of Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Maurya Empire?
questions.hint: He was guided by Chanakya.

questions.prompt: Which emperor embraced Buddhism after the Kalinga War?
questions.hint: He belonged to the Maurya dynasty.

questions.prompt: Who wrote the national song 'Vande Mataram'?
questions.hint: He also wrote the novel Anandamath.

questions.prompt: Which freedom fighter was popularly known as Iron Man of India?
questions.hint: He played a major role in integrating princely states.

questions.prompt: Who was the last Viceroy of British India?
questions.hint: He oversaw the partition and transfer of power.

questions.prompt: Which event in 1905 triggered the Swadeshi movement?
questions.hint: It divided a major eastern province.

questions.prompt: Which city was the capital of British India before New Delhi?
questions.hint: It is now the capital of West Bengal.

questions.prompt: Who designed the Indian national flag in its early form?
questions.hint: His surname is Venkayya.

questions.prompt: Which movement was started by Gandhi in 1930 with a march to the sea?
questions.hint: It protested a tax on a common kitchen item.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Indian National Congress along with other leaders in 1885?
questions.hint: He was a retired British civil servant.

questions.prompt: What was the name of Bhagat Singh's close revolutionary associate who was executed with him?
questions.hint: One of them was Rajguru.

questions.prompt: Which 1857 uprising is often called the First War of Independence?
questions.hint: It began as a sepoy mutiny.

questions.prompt: Who was the Rani of Jhansi?
questions.hint: She became a symbol of resistance in 1857.

questions.prompt: Which Indian leader is known as Lokmanya Tilak?
questions.hint: He said, 'Swaraj is my birthright.'

questions.prompt: Which ancient university in Bihar was a famous centre of learning?
questions.hint: Its ruins are now a UNESCO site.

questions.prompt: Who wrote the Indian national anthem 'Jana Gana Mana'?
questions.hint: He was also the first Asian Nobel laureate.

questions.prompt: Which constitutionally important day is celebrated on 26 January in India?
questions.hint: It marks the day India became a republic.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Satya Shodhak Samaj?
questions.hint: He was a major social reformer from Maharashtra.

questions.prompt: Who is known as the Nightingale of India?
questions.hint: She was also the first woman president of the Indian National Congress.

questions.prompt: Which freedom fighter was popularly called Frontier Gandhi?
questions.hint: His first name was Khan Abdul.

questions.prompt: Which act passed in 1935 gave provinces more autonomy in British India?
questions.hint: It has the words Government and India in its name.

questions.prompt: At which place did Gandhi first launch a major satyagraha in India in 1917?
questions.hint: It was related to indigo farmers in Bihar.

questions.prompt: Which session of the Indian National Congress declared Poorna Swaraj as the goal?
questions.hint: It was held in Lahore in 1929.

questions.prompt: Who was the founder of the Mauryan advisor text Arthashastra?
questions.hint: He was also known as Kautilya.

questions.prompt: Which famous traveler visited India during the reign of Harshavardhana?
questions.hint: He came from China.

questions.prompt: Which empire is associated with rulers like Krishnadevaraya?
questions.hint: Its capital was Hampi.

questions.prompt: Who established the Khalsa in 1699?
questions.hint: He was the tenth Sikh Guru.

questions.prompt: Which battle in 1757 laid the foundation of British rule in India?
questions.hint: It involved Robert Clive and Siraj ud-Daulah.

questions.prompt: Which battle in 1764 strengthened British power in Bengal and beyond?
questions.hint: It came after Plassey.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Brahmo Samaj?
questions.hint: He was an important social and religious reformer in Bengal.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Arya Samaj?
questions.hint: His first name was Swami.

questions.prompt: Who was the first Indian woman to become president of the Indian National Congress?
questions.hint: She was Annie.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Home Rule League in India along with Bal Gangadhar Tilak?
questions.hint: She was of Irish origin.

questions.prompt: Which pact was signed between Gandhi and Irwin in 1931?
questions.hint: It ended the first phase of Civil Disobedience.

questions.prompt: Which prison term is associated with Jawaharlal Nehru's book The Discovery of India?
questions.hint: He wrote it while imprisoned in Ahmednagar Fort.

questions.prompt: What was the original name of Swami Vivekananda before he became a monk?
questions.hint: His first name was Narendra.

questions.prompt: Who was the founder of the Ramakrishna Mission?
questions.hint: He spoke at the Parliament of the World's Religions in Chicago.

questions.prompt: Which king was known for the slogan 'Hindavi Swarajya' and founded the Maratha kingdom?
questions.hint: He was crowned at Raigad.

questions.prompt: Who was the first President of India?
questions.hint: He shared his first name with Prasad.

questions.prompt: Which movement followed the suspension of the Non-Cooperation Movement and aimed at entering legislative councils?
questions.hint: It was founded by C. R. Das and Motilal Nehru.

questions.prompt: Which British commission was opposed with the slogan 'Simon Go Back'?
questions.hint: It arrived in India in 1928.

questions.prompt: Who was the first Indian Governor-General of independent India?
questions.hint: He was also known as Rajaji.

questions.prompt: Which historic site in Delhi served as the main residence of Mughal emperors and later became a symbol of freedom on 15 August?
questions.hint: The Prime Minister hoists the flag there every Independence Day.
